Defendant Pj Helicopters Inc'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Abate / Stay Action Due To Another Action Pending; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Aug-04-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Elise Sanguinetti, Esq. (510-629-4877; elise@aswtlawyers.com) of Arias Sanguinetti
Wang & Torrijos LLP for Plaintiff, Erica Wasdin.
Jonathan Hembree, esq. (626-765-3000; jhembree@cunninghamswain.com) of Cunningham Swaim, LLP for Defendant/Moving Party, P.J. Helicopters, Inc.
Mike Danko, Esq. (650-453-3600; mdanko@dankolaw.com) for Other, Ashley King as personal representative of the estate of Jimmy Wasdin.
Jennifer Dodge, Esq. (310-372-3344; jdodgelaw@jenniferdodgelaw.com) for Defendant, Pacific Gas and Electric Company.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT P.J. HELICOPTERS, INC.'S Motion To Abate / Stay Action Due To Another Action Pending:

Defendant P.J. Helicopters, Inc.'s motion to stay or abate this action is granted. A wrongful death action may be brought by the decedent's heirs or by the personal representative of the decedent's estate on behalf of the heirs, but not by both. (Code Civ. Proc. sec. 377.60; Adams v. Superior Court (2011) 196 Cal.App.4th 71, 76-77.) A plaintiff who is suing as the decedent's representative on behalf of the heirs need not join the heirs as parties to the action. (Adams, 196 Cal.App.4th at 76-78; see also Code Civ. Proc. sec. 369(a)(1).) A personal representative in such a case acts as a statutory trustee to recover damages for the benefit of the heirs, and is their fiduciary. (Id. at 77-78.) "One advantage to having the personal representative bring the suit is that control of the lawsuit is centralized. Another is that no guardian ad litem need be appointed for minor heirs, and no petition for compromise of the claim of a minor need be filed. . . . . It can be more convenient to have a personal representative bring a wrongful death action on behalf of the heirs if the heirs are numerous, distant, or are not willing to join as plaintiffs." (Id. at 78 fn. 8 (citation omitted).) Here, abatement of this action is appropriate in view of the earlier-filed action by the decedent's personal representative, Ashley King, as Personal Representative of the Estate of Jimmy Wasdin, Deceased v. Pacific Gas and Electric Company, et al., CGC-20-587976. Plaintiff's contention that the two actions should be consolidated is groundless in light of the foregoing authority. (See Adams, 196 Cal.App.4th at 77 ["[e]ither the administratix or the heirs, but not both, may sue, and if the administratix sues, the heirs may not" (citation omitted)].)

Order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)
